Southampton Community Newsletter

Dear Kathryn,

We hope you're well. This month we are sharing a roundup of upcoming local activities, wellbeing resources, and support services available to residents across Southampton.

Upcoming events near you

  • Spring Social at Shirley Community Centre — 12 April, 10:30–13:00. Tea, light lunch and gentle activities. More details and registration: Southampton City Council events.
  • Local Nature Walks — weekly walks from Riverside Park. Family friendly. Info and meeting points: Parks & open spaces.
  • Free IT drop-in — help using email, video calls and online services. Book a slot at the Age UK Southampton page.

Health & wellbeing

If you have questions about seasonal vaccinations, local health clinics, or wellbeing services, the NHS has up-to-date guidance: NHS - Live Well. For advice on staying active and social in later life, see Age UK.

Support and advice

Our local advice teams can assist with transport schemes, concessionary travel, and benefits guidance. Contact Southampton City Council's residents' services for personalised support:

View local services & contact

Get involved

Volunteers are always welcome at community centres and local events. If you'd like to volunteer or suggest activities for the neighbourhood, please reply to this email or complete the short form at: southampton.gov.uk/volunteering.

If you would prefer not to receive these monthly updates, you can manage your subscriptions at southampton.gov.uk/contact-us or reply to this message with "unsubscribe" in the subject line.

Kind regards,
Emma Clarke
Community Engagement Officer

Southampton City Council
Civic Centre, Southampton. For general enquiries call 023 8083 3000 or visit www.southampton.gov.uk.

Southampton City Council — Community News

Dear Kathryn,

We hope you are well. This month’s community update brings together local services, upcoming events and support available for residents in Southampton.

Events & Activities

Join neighbours for a friendly coffee morning and information session for older residents. These gatherings are free and take place at the Shirley Community Centre:

  • Monthly Tea & Talk — First Wednesday of each month, 10:30am
  • Gentle Exercise Class — Tuesdays, 11:00am
  • Computer Help Desk — Fridays, 2:00pm to 4:00pm (bring your device)

View community centre details

Support & Services

If you or someone you care for needs extra support, the council and local charities can help with transport, health advice, and home support.

  • Information on support for older residents: Age UK
  • Health advice and appointments: NHS
  • Local council support pages: Southampton City Council — Health & Social Care

Transport & Accessibility

If transport is a concern, concessionary travel information and community transport options are available. For up-to-date bus routes and concession fares, visit:

First Bus — South West | Southampton travel information

Contact & Feedback

If you have questions or would like assistance registering for an event, contact our Community Support team. We’re happy to help arrange assistance or answer any questions about local services.

Community Support — Southampton City Council
Email: community@southampton.gov.uk
Phone: 023 8083 3000
